rulings.Case Title: SARAH STROTHER VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 4, 2008, line 5. PLAINTIFF SARAH STROTHER'S Motion To Tax Costs is denied based on the court's ruling granting defendant fees and costs under CCP Section 1038(a). =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: SARAH STROTHER VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Awarding Defense Costs, Including Atty And Witness Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 4, 2008, line 5. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S Motion Awarding Defense Costs, Including Attorney And Witness Fees. The motion is granted. Plaintiff's fall is attributable solidly and exclusively to her admittedly not looking where she was going. Her vision of the two steps was unobstructed. The two steps were open and obvious and were the very stairs plaintiff had ascended going into the building. No one could reasonably believe that plaintiff had a valid claim based on the undisputed facts. Pursuant to CCP section 1038(a), the Court awards defendant its attorneys fees in the sum of $41,236 and costs $894 for depositions, $1,286 for subpoena costs, $3,620 for expert costs and copying costs of $5,489. Although CCP section 1038(b) does not specify copying costs, the breadth of CCP Section 1038(a) makes it reasonable to award such costs in this case for the technical drawings make clear the limitations of plaintiff's claim. =(302/PJM/PB)
